author: Nishida, Tatsuo
title: Seikago no kenkyu [A study of the Hsi-Hsia language: reconstruction of the Hsi-Hsia language and decipherment of the Hsi-Hsia script]
notes:
in Japanese. Glosses translated into english by Shiro Yabu.  Large source; enter in pieces?

JP 6/26/97:  Looks like it's body parts that have been entered.  There has been, to my eye, no change in transcription; that is, source and database transcriptions are identical.  The words have been taken from two places, and labeled (language name) as "Tangut 1" and "Tangut 2".  1 comes from vol. 2, Appendix 1, pp. 303-507.  2 comes from vol. 1, pp. 205-6.  The former is a dictionary ordered by character radical.  The latter comes from glosses to some sentences.  The order of entry is hard to figure out.  Tangut 1 contains within it (when ordered by RN) two very roughly alphabetical (by English gloss) sub-lists.  That is, it goes roughly from A to Z, then roughly from A to Z again.  Tangut 2 is also strangely ordered.  I estimate total forms to be 3500-4000, of which 409 have been entered.  The source is in Japanese, and is shelved alphabetically in Room 2.  English glosses (for Tangut 1) evidently come from penciled-in notes in the source.
